title: Real-Robot Lab Infra
description: Engineering stack for teleoperation, data collection, and robot-side validation
summary: This is the engineering base that keeps weekly research work grounded in real data collection and real-robot validation.
asset_caption: Hardware matrix: ready systems, dual-Franka same-computer control, and planned glove / tracker teleoperation stack.
details: Ready inventory baseline: Linker FFG, UDCAP data glove, Cobot Magic / Piper-X, Linker Hand L6, and Daimon UMI.

Missing wrapper: Linker TA + Linker FFG still needs a local control wrapper before it can be treated as ready infra. The wrapper should expose connection/config, state readback, command open/close or position/force targets, error handling, and a smoke verifier.
Completed today: ARX / Fangzhou Wuxian real-robot infra setup.

ARX SOP received: Operation of ARX Robot Arm for Drag Teaching and Teleoperation Data Collection, JZ/SOP/ROB/001, issued 2026-05-23. It covers trained-operator responsibility, power-up, drag teaching, teleoperation, shutdown, and immediate stop conditions.
Progress on 2026-05-18: dual Franka arms can now be controlled from the same computer.
Progress on 2026-05-21: Gello-controlled dual Franka + Wuji Hand setup is complete. Current control treats the Wuji dexterous hand as a one-DoF gripper via Gello trigger open/close mapping.
Demo waterproofing consumables purchased: disposable nitrile gloves and finger cots for short demo protection.

Next input stack: complete MANUS + VIVE as one task: glove streaming, VIVE tracker listing, external/time-sync record, retargeting replay verifier, and ACE Teleop wrist-ring fallback if VIVE is skipped.
Before Feibo uses the Franka dexterous-hand setup for egg-cracking tests, cable-manage the setup with organizers / zip ties, clear an empty work area, and waterproof the outside area.

Teacher demo request: plan a dexterous-hand knife-cutting-meat demo, but treat it as a safety-gated manipulation demo rather than a casual kitchen task. Define fixture, blade guard / boundary, low-speed rehearsal, emergency-stop coverage, and multi-person supervision before any live cutting.
FACTR procurement route: purchase through Jingte Intelligent; keep the dashboard task focused on procurement path, gravity compensation, and force-feedback safety value.
Wrist tracking fallback: if VIVE Tracker is not used for wrist pose, evaluate an ACE Teleop-style wrist ring to replace the Gello-end trigger as the wrist / intent input device.
MANUS + VIVE merge: hardware verifier requirements are folded into the single MANUS + VIVE task; do not track a separate verifier TODO.
risks_decisions: 不同设备的驱动、SDK、ROS topic 和日志格式需要尽早统一，否则后面每条数据都会带维护成本。
手套、VIVE、相机、机器人状态要共享时间同步和外参标定策略。
真机 infra 必须保留安全边界：限位、急停、低速验证、碰撞恢复和操作记录；后续真机调试至少两人在场，一人专门看急停。
Gello 是被动主臂，软件无法在主臂端实现真正的重力补偿；主臂失手下落可能把 Franka 带向桌面，需要机械重力补偿、阻尼 / 刹车或 FACTR 这类带力反馈的双边遥操作方案。
一次性丁腈手套和指套已经买好，可用于短 demo 防水保护；但 Franka 灵巧手做敲鸡蛋等测试前，仍必须先处理杂线、固定线缆、清空工作区，并在工作区外做防水。

ARX drag-teaching / teleoperation collection must use the SOP as an operation gate: secure base, gripper, controller, power, cameras, and connections; clear the work area; test slow response first; keep people outside the workspace; stop immediately on abnormal resistance, sound, vibration, communication delay, unstable control, or unexpected motion.
工程状态需要和研究项目解耦：infra 卡片只追踪设备可用性，算法结果留在各项目卡片里。

Teacher knife demo safety: using a dexterous hand to hold a knife and cut meat must start from fixture / dummy-material rehearsal, speed limits, blade-safe workspace, and explicit abort conditions; do not combine sharp-tool trials with single-person operation.
FACTR procurement route: FACTR purchase should go through Jingte Intelligent rather than the slow school process, while xArm / already-started school items remain separate.
